the principle embodied here work satisfactorily? We are as
sured on good authority that the agreement comes true within
an orb of three degrees. This itself is enough to show that the
entire method admits of an error of about ten minutes in the
rectified time of birth. Further, take an example of a horo-'
scope: Ascdt. is Aries 3deg. 15'; Sun in Capricorn 8 deg. H';
Moon in Capricorn 19 deg.58'; and M.C. 266 deg. 49'. The
equation 5 (a) alone fits in here; and I get the longitude 278
deg.23' which falls within that of the Sun. But since I can
expect an error of three degrees, the time of birth can as well
be ten minutes earlier or later. This is surely beyond any
comprehension.

one half of this amount.
